arts.ttl: Brighton vs Chelsea – FA Cup match preview and team news
arts.sum: Brighton and Chelsea battle it out this weekend for a place in the fifth round of the FA Cup. Coincidentally, Saturday night’s fixture will be replicated next weekend as the two sides face off in the Premier League on Valentine’s Day. arts.sum: Liverpool are understood to be closely monitoring Feyenoord defender David Hancko as a potential transfer target for the summer. arts.ttl: Chelsea seduced by 12-G/A Premier League forward, Blues plan to offer £42m
arts.sum: Chelsea are keen on signing the Bournemouth attacker Antoine Semenyo. The 25-year-old Ghanaian winger has been quite impressive in the Premier League this season and he has 12 goal contributions in all competitions. According to Fichajes, Chelsea are seduced by his dynamism and versatility. He has been linked with Arsenal recently. arts.ttl: Brighton vs Chelsea preview: Guiu adds to Blues' injury concerns
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca revealed Marc Guiu suffered a long-term injury in Chelsea’s 2-1 Premier League win over West Ham on Monday. “It doesn’t look like a short injury and exactly how long I don’t know.

arts.ttl: We simulated Brighton vs Chelsea to predict FA Cup fourth round clash
arts.sum: Chelsea have won their last five games against Brighton, most recently a 4-2 win in the Premier League where Cole Palmer scored all four before half-time. A convincing 5-0 win over League Two Morecambe secured Chelsea's spot in the FA Cup fourth round last month, setting up the clash with Brighton after the Seagulls beat Norwich City 4-0.

arts.ttl: Chelsea prepare €50m bid for Premier League’s biggest SURPRISE
arts.sum: The Blues have their eyes fixed on Bournemouth attacker Antoine Semenyo, with Enzo Maresca’s side now ready to spend big on the versatile forward, who has emerged as one of the players of the season in England’s top-flight. Semenyo has an Estimated Transfer Value (ETV) of €37.5m, and with this figure having exploded since the start of the season, Chelsea’s budget to sign the player, who is under contract with Bournemouth until 2029, appears reasonable.
